name: burrow-toml-schema
description: burrow.toml is parsed via smol-toml + Zod (BurrowTomlSchema) in src/schemas/burrow-toml.ts; loader is src/config/burrow-toml-loader.ts which returns null when absent and throws ValidationError when malformed. Every section is .optional() at the top level so an empty file is valid; sections themselves are .strict() to surface unknown-key typos. [[agents]] entries reuse AgentConfigSchema.partial() with id required so they can patch a built-in (id-only) or define a new declarative runtime (full row).

content: AgentConfigSchema fields are camelCase (matching SPEC §12.3): displayName, promptDelivery, outputFormat, supportsResume, forwardCredentials. Top-level burrow.toml schemas (project, sandbox, env, git) are snake_case (default_branch, allowed_domains). Agent-block fields are the exception. When adding a new optional toggle to AgentConfigSchema, prefer z.boolean().optional() over .default(true): .default() makes the Zod OUTPUT type required, which forces every test constructing an AgentConfig literal (declarative.test.ts, registry.test.ts) to add the field. With .optional(), the consumer checks 'agent.forwardCredentials === false' for opt-out and undefined means 'use the runtime's contribution'.

src/server/openapi/spec.ts errorResponse(label) wraps the label in backticks itself ('Error envelope (`${label}`).'). Don't embed backticks in the label — they nest awkwardly in the rendered description. Keep labels as 'snake_code — short context' plain text.

Admin routes (POST /admin/drain) are intentionally NOT in ROUTE_PATTERNS (src/server/routes.ts) — the spec.test.ts ROUTE_PATTERNS ⊆ spec check would otherwise force them into the canonical mirrored table. They mount via buildAdminRoutes() (src/server/admin.ts) and appear in the OpenAPI spec (src/server/openapi/spec.ts) under the 'admin' tag without being in ROUTE_PATTERNS.

Drain-gated routes (POST /burrows, POST /burrows/:id/runs) declare a 503 worker_draining response in src/server/openapi/spec.ts. Other admin-aware routes (reads, lifecycle, inbox, streams) don't get a 503 because the drain gate (gateMirroredRoutes in src/server/routes.ts) only wraps the two create handlers.
Unified boolean query param grammar across HTTP endpoints
